content:
7 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

My “fast” signals migration made my app slower.
Not a little slower. My dashboard went from smooth to visibly stuttering, and the profiler showed one computed running 412 times for a single click.
Signals were supposed to fix this. The framework was not the problem.
I was.
Over three months I collected seven mistakes, each one silent, each one shipping to production without a single error or warning. I made every one of them personally, which means I can show you exactly what each one looks like, what it costs in real numbers, and the small fix that removes it.
Count how many live in your codebase right now. My guess is at least three.
Mistake 1: Mutating the Object Inside a Signal
This is the one everybody makes in week one, and it is the sneakiest because the code looks completely reasonable.
export class Cart {
items = signal<Item[]>([]); add(item: Item) {
this.items().push(item);
}
}The item goes into the array. The array lives inside the signal. And the screen shows nothing, because the signal never knew.
